intentional_test_features: Textbook acute myocardial infarction: prolonged (>20 min) crushing retrosternal chest pain at rest, radiating to the left arm and jaw, with diaphoresis, vomiting and a sense of impending doom - the AI Doctor must recognise a STEMI and act immediately
Aspirin IS appropriate here (contrast with aortic dissection): advise a chewed 300 mg aspirin unless contraindicated while awaiting the ambulance
Time-critical reperfusion: 'time is muscle' - immediate 000 with pre-notification for primary PCI (cath lab) is required, and the highest early risk is ventricular fibrillation/cardiac arrest
Autonomic shock features (ashen, diaphoretic, vomiting, weak pulses, borderline BP/SpO2) indicate a large infarct and haemodynamic strain
CPR safety-netting: the spouse must be ready to start chest compressions if the patient becomes unresponsive

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
